headers.//: Images are addressed by a stable name and effectively never change in place; a day of hard caching with a long stale-while-revalidate keeps them instant while still letting a swapped photograph propagate.

The CSS and JS are served from unversioned paths (/css/styles.css), so a long max-age has no way to be invalidated: a returning visitor keeps the old stylesheet against the new HTML — which renders the site badly — until it expires. Revalidate every load; the answer is a 304 on a connection that is already open for the HTML.
